content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 20, 1987

James E. Williams, Executive Director
Sacramento Area Council of Governments
106 K Street, Ste. 200
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-87-067

Dear Mr. Williams:

This is in response to your letter asking whether Joe Benatar
must file a statement of economic interests (Form 730) as an
Alternate Director of the Sacramento Area Council of Governments
(SACOG).

A review of SACOG's conflict of interest code indicates that
Alternate Directors are required to file statements of economic
interests disclosing all investments in business entities and
sources of income which are located in or doing business in
SACOG's jurisdiction, and all interests in real property located
in or within two miles of SACOG's jurisdiction or within five miles
of any land owned or used by SACOG. (Appendix to the Conflict of
Interest Code for the Sacramento Area Council of Governments, copy
enclosed.)

There appears to be nothing in the code which exempts
Alternate Directors who have not actually served on the Council
from the requirement to file disclosure statements. However, as a
member of the Sutter County Board of Supervisors, Mr. Benatar is
required under Government Code Section 87200 to file a statement
of economic interests (Form 721) each year and he may complete one
Form 721 and include all information required to be disclosed for
both positions. He may then file that statement, including an
original signature, with both Sutter County and SACOG. However,
because his jurisdiction for reporting under Section 87200 is
limited to Sutter County and the jurisdiction of SACOG appears to
be much broader, Mr. Benatar may wish to file a separate Form 730
with SACOG.

If you have additional questions, please do not hesitate to
contact me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

By: Carla Wardlow
Political Reform Consultant

Enclosure

FPPC
FEB 27 10 20 AH '87

February 25, 1987

Betty Brown, Legal Documents Examiner
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Ms. Brown:

Enclosed is a copy of a letter from Alternate Director Joe
Benatar regarding the need to file an Annual Statement due
April 1, 1987.

We would appreciate your opinion as to whether or not he is
required to file Statements of Economic Interests as an
Alternate Director of the Sacramento Area Council of
Governments.

Your prompt response will be appreciated.

Sincerely,

JAMES E. WILLIAMS
EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR

JW:jrc
Encls.

COUNTY OF SUTTER
BOARD OF SUPERVISORS

JOE BENATAR, DISTRICT 2

SUTTER COUNTY OFFICE BUILDING
463 SECOND STREET
YUBA CITY, CALIFORNIA 95991
916-741-7106

February 12, 1987

SACOG
106 K Street, Suite 200
Sacramento, CA 95814

Attn: James E. Williams, Executive Director

Dear Mr. Williams:

I am in receipt of your memorandum dated January 29, 1987,
with regard to filing Form 730, Statement of Economic Interest.

I was appointed to SACOG in the capacity as Alternate Director,
but I never attended any meetings. Since I never assumed the
position of serving, I did not file a 730 Form.

If you have any questions, please contact my office at (916)
741-7106.

Sincerely,

Joe Benatar
Supervisor, District Two

/jb

REGULAR MEETINGS OF THE BOARD OF SUPERVISORS HELD IN BOARD CHAMBERS, 463 SECOND STREET.

TUESDAYS, 7:00 P.M.

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

February 27, 1987


James E. Williams
Sacramento Area Council
of Governments
P.O. Box 808
Sacramento, CA  95804

Re:  87-067

Dear Mr. Williams:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on February 27, 1987 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days. You also should be aware that your
letter and our response are public records which may be
disclosed to the public upon receipt of a proper request for
disclosure.

Very truly yours,

[signature]

Jeanne Pritchard
Chief
Technical Assistance and Analysis
Division

sections.question: Whether Joe Benatar must file a statement of economic interests (Form 730) as an Alternate Director of the Sacramento Area Council of Governments (SACOG).
sections.conclusion: A review of SACOG's conflict of interest code indicates that Alternate Directors are required to file statements of economic interests disclosing all investments in business entities and sources of income which are located in or doing business in SACOG's jurisdiction, and all interests in real property located in or within two miles of SACOG's jurisdiction or within two miles of any land owned or used by SACOG. There appears to be nothing in the code which exempts Alternate Directors who have not actually served on the Council from the requirement to file disclosure statements. However, as a member of the Sutter County Board of Supervisors, Mr. Benatar is required under Government Code section 87200 to file a statement of economic interests (Form 721) each year and he may complete one Form 721 and include all information required to be disclosed for both positions. He may then file that statement, including an original signature, with both Sutter County and SACOG. However, because his jurisdiction for reporting under Section 87200 is limited to Sutter County and the jurisdiction of SACOG appears to be much broader, Mr. Benatar may wish to file a separate Form 730 with SACOG.
sections.question_synthetic: Must an Alternate Director of SACOG file a Form 730 statement of economic interests, particularly when that person has not actually served on the Council?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Alternate Directors of SACOG must file Form 730 statements of economic interests regardless of whether they have actually served on the Council. As Benatar is also a county supervisor subject to Form 721 requirements, he may file a single combined form with both entities or file separate disclosures if SACOG's broader jurisdiction warrants additional reporting.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Complete FPPC advice letter with clear question and conclusion sections. Document includes transmittal letter from Technical Assistance Division, the advice response from General Counsel, and related correspondence from the requester and the subject individual.

QUESTION: Whether Joe Benatar must file a statement of economic interests (Form 730) as an Alternate Director of the Sacramento Area Council of Governments (SACOG).

CONCLUSION: A review of SACOG's conflict of interest code indicates that Alternate Directors are required to file statements of economic interests disclosing all investments in business entities and sources of income which are located in or doing business in SACOG's jurisdiction, and all interests in real property located in or within two miles of SACOG's jurisdiction or within two miles of any land owned or used by SACOG. There appears to be nothing in the code which exempts Alternate Directors who have not actually served on the Council from the requirement to file disclosure statements. However, as a member of the Sutter County Board of Supervisors, Mr. Benatar is required under Government Code section 87200 to file a statement of economic interests (Form 721) each year and he may complete one Form 721 and include all information required to be disclosed for both positions. He may then file that statement, including an original signature, with both Sutter County and SACOG. However, because his jurisdiction for reporting under Section 87200 is limited to Sutter County and the jurisdiction of SACOG appears to be much broader, Mr. Benatar may wish to file a separate Form 730 with SACOG.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises that Joe Benatar, appointed as an Alternate Director to SACOG, must file a Form 730 statement of economic interests even though he never attended meetings. As a Sutter County Board member, he may file a combined Form 721 with both entities or separate disclosures depending on jurisdictional considerations.
